Politics.headline: Trump's fixation on White House ballroom is increasing, Post analysis finds
Politics.detail: The president has publicly highlighted his ballroom project on roughly a third of the days this year, showcasing his continued focus on the renovation.

Politics.headline: Congress approves 10-day extension for warrantless surveillance law
Politics.detail: Congress has approved a short-term extension of the controversial warrantless surveillance law, ensuring its continuation for another 10 days.

Politics.headline: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz; says both sides are "far" from peace deal
Politics.detail: Iran's parliamentary speaker announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, stating that it will remain closed unless the U.S. lifts its blockade, signaling difficulties in ongoing peace negotiations.

Politics.headline: Scoop: Trump convenes Iran situation room meeting amid renewed Hormuz crisis
Politics.detail: President Trump convened a situation room meeting to address the renewed crisis in the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting the escalating tensions in the region.

Politics.headline: Starmer would have blocked Mandelson role over vetting failure, says Lammy
Politics.detail: Labour's David Lammy stated that Keir Starmer would have blocked Peter Mandelson's appointment if he had been aware of the failed vetting process, indicating the severity of the situation.

Finance & Economy.headline: Turkiye woos investors amid Iran war fallout in Gulf economies
Finance & Economy.detail: Turkish officials are promoting Istanbul as a regional financial hub amid the war's fallout on the Gulf economies.

Finance & Economy.headline: Oil prices drop 9% and Wall Street rallies to a record after Iran reopens the Strait of Hormuz
Finance & Economy.detail: Oil prices dropped back to where they were in the early days of the Iran war, and U.S. stocks raced to another record after Iran said the Strait of Hormuz is open again for commercial tankers.

Finance & Economy.headline: Carney's pitch to unlock trillions in global investment
Finance & Economy.detail: Prime Minister Mark Carney has invited 100 of the biggest investment firms in the world to an Invest in Canada Summit this fall, aiming to unlock trillions of dollars in investments in Canada.

Finance & Economy.headline: UK seeks EU deals on steel and EVs in push for closer economic ties
Finance & Economy.detail: The UK is seeking deals with the EU on steel and electric vehicles as part of an effort to forge closer economic ties. Labour's approach to closer EU ties must address the damage of Brexit.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump faces surging inflation report, fueled by Iran conflict
Finance & Economy.detail: Trump is contending with a surging inflation report that has been exacerbated by the conflict with Iran.

Technology.headline: 'Your resume is worth nothing in 2026': Viral post exposes how random tech hiring has become
Technology.detail: A viral social media post highlights the brutal hiring reality at a US startup, where a founder received 6,840 applications for three positions, sparking a debate on the significance of elite degrees and the role of luck in job hunting.

Technology.headline: FCC Router Ban Targets Chinese Hackers. I Bet Yours Wasn't Even Made There
Technology.detail: The FCC is implementing a ban on certain foreign-made routers due to security concerns, particularly those that could be exploited by Chinese hackers.

Technology.headline: Tech diaspora in U.S. marks 75 years of IIT amid concerns over AI, immigration curbs
Technology.detail: As the Indian Institutes of Technology (IITs) celebrate 75 years, IIT alumni in the U.S. are concerned about unfavorable immigration policies and the impact of AI on the job market, potentially reversing the 'brain drain'.

Technology.headline: White House chief of staff meets with Anthropic CEO over its new AI technology
Technology.detail: White House chief of staff Susie Wiles met with Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei to discuss the company's new AI model, Mythos.

Technology.headline: SpaceX Snipes at Blue Origin's Starlink Challenger Over Interference Risks
Technology.detail: SpaceX has raised concerns about potential interference risks from Blue Origin's proposed Starlink challenger.

Science.headline: A New Force of Nature Is Reshaping the Planet, Study Finds
Science.detail: Human societies have reshaped the planet through cultural and social innovations, unlocking extraordinary power to transform Earth and improve human life, from early fire use to today's global supply chains. Scientists have also developed a fuel cell that uses microbes in soil to produce electricity, capable of powering underground sensors for monitoring moisture or detecting touch without needing batteries or solar panels.

Science.headline: Voyager 1 Matters, and Why NASA Just Switched Part of It Off
Science.detail: NASA has shut down one of Voyager 1's remaining science instruments to extend its life as it runs out of power nearly half a century after its launch. On April 17, mission engineers deactivated the Low-energy Charged Particles experiment (LECP), which has been crucial in mapping the structure of interstellar space.

Science.headline: Shrinking American science
Science.detail: Federal grants from the National Institutes of Health are decreasing, impacting researchers and their projects, with the Trump administration having cut the number of grants, especially those focused on women's health, cancer, and mental health. STEM and health employees at science-focused agencies experienced nearly 15,000 job cuts between January 2025 and February 2026, outpacing cuts among other federal workers.

Science.headline: CSCU and State Officials Launch Connecticut's Quantum Science Center
Science.detail: Connecticut has launched a Quantum Science Center at Southern Connecticut State University, which will drive student opportunity, workforce development, and statewide innovation. The center is supported by the state's Tech Talent Accelerator and a National Science Foundation EPIIC grant, enhancing academic-industry partnerships in fields like quantum information science and advanced manufacturing.

Science.headline: Ethiopia to Host the SIOP Africa 2028
Science.detail: Ethiopia is set to host the SIOP Africa 2028, marking a significant event for pediatric oncology in the region. The conference will bring together experts and researchers to discuss advancements and challenges in treating childhood cancer in Africa.

Health.headline: Trump signs order to accelerate access to psychedelic drug treatments
Health.detail: U.S. President Donald Trump signed an executive order intended to speed up access to medical research and treatment based on psychedelic drugs like ibogaine, potentially reclassifying them after successful trials and dedicating $50 million to federal research. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has championed the use of such drugs as alternative treatments for mental health conditions.

Health.headline: Proposed industrial wastewater rules 'completely inadequate'
Health.detail: Proposed rules in North Carolina would require public treatment works and manufacturers to monitor for the industrial solvent 1,4-dioxane in wastewater discharges. Concerns have been raised that the costs to treat 1,4-dioxane are staggering, making source control the only practical path forward.

Health.headline: In CT, doctors now sue patients most over medical bills, surpassing hospitals
Health.detail: A recent investigation reveals that doctors, dentists, and ambulance companies in Connecticut are now suing patients for unpaid medical bills more often than hospitals. Non-hospital providers account for over 80% of healthcare debt collection cases in Connecticut courts.

Health.headline: Lawmakers pressed to address Michigan's Black maternal health disparities
Health.detail: Advocates in Michigan are urging lawmakers to address the state's racial disparities in maternal healthcare, where Black women die during childbirth more than 2.5 times as often as white women. Proposed legislation aims to update data tracking, require insurance coverage for midwife services, and bar discrimination based on pregnancy or lactating status.

Health.headline: Are you a woman who makes life easier for everyone else? Beware – you could endanger your health
Health.detail: A new claim suggests that women who are too nice risk getting an autoimmune disease. The article discusses the potential health risks associated with women who consistently prioritize the needs of others.

Sports.headline: Falcons trade Ruke Orhorhoro to the Jaguars in exchange for fellow DT Maason Smith
Sports.detail: The Atlanta Falcons traded Ruke Orhorhoro to the Jacksonville Jaguars for Maason Smith, both defensive tackles.

Sports.headline: Wild rookie goalie Jesper Wallstedt stands out winning his NHL playoffs debut over the Stars
Sports.detail: Jesper Wallstedt, the Wild's rookie goalie, had a remarkable NHL playoffs debut, leading his team to victory over the Stars.

Sports.headline: Johnson's go-ahead 2-run double in the 6th helps Rockies beat Dodgers 4-3
Sports.detail: Johnson's two-run double in the sixth inning propelled the Rockies to a 4-3 victory over the Dodgers.

Sports.headline: Tedesco makes big Origin statement as Roosters seal comeback win in 46-year first
Sports.detail: James Tedesco showcased a stunning performance, leading the Roosters to a comeback victory and making a statement for his State of Origin hopes.

Sports.headline: Inside the Eels' monumental Dogs upset... and how the '86 winners sparked dream turnaround
Sports.detail: The Parramatta Eels pulled off a significant upset against the Bulldogs, inspired by the 1986 NSWRL premiership team, focusing on defense and intensity to secure the win.

Entertainment.headline: Deepika Padukone and Ranveer Singh announce second pregnancy
Entertainment.detail: The couple shared the joyful news on Sunday, April 19, 2026, with a picture of their daughter Dua holding a positive pregnancy test. Deepika and Ranveer, who married in 2018, welcomed their first child in 2024 and are now expecting their second.

Entertainment.headline: Eddie Murphy receives life achievement award by AFI
Entertainment.detail: Eddie Murphy was honored with the American Film Institute's life achievement award at a star-studded ceremony, drawing a standing ovation. The event celebrated his contributions to comedy and film.

Entertainment.headline: Golden Retriever wearing a hat went viral devouring a full hot dog at the ballpark
Entertainment.detail: A video of a Golden Retriever wearing a hat enthusiastically eating a hot dog at a baseball game has become a viral sensation. The Aloha State Daily reported on the feel-good story as part of their 'Daily Smile' feature.

Entertainment.headline: Japan's latest tourist destination: hair salons
Entertainment.detail: A growing number of tourists are visiting Japan specifically to experience the country's hair salons, drawn by the expertise in treatments like Japanese hair-straightening. American teenager Lily Romero, traveling from London, visited a Tokyo salon for a four-hour straightening treatment.

Entertainment.headline: Obama meets Mamdani in New York City ahead of a preschool reading event
Entertainment.detail: Former President Barack Obama met privately with New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani before they both participated in a reading event for preschoolers in the Bronx. The event aimed to promote early childhood literacy.

Crime & Justice.headline: How Michigan man plotted mass murder at 2 nearby locations, officials say
Crime & Justice.detail: Mack Davis, 23, of Owosso, Michigan, plotted a mass murder at two nearby locations, becoming infatuated with other mass murderers and planning to conduct his own attack, according to court documents; he was sentenced to seven years in federal prison. Davis drafted a list of weapons and tactical gear, surveilled churches, synagogues, mosques, schools, hospitals, and supermarkets, and wrote about his hate for the world and its people.

Crime & Justice.headline: Hundreds trying to storm Wisconsin beagle research facility met with rubber bullets and pepper spray
Crime & Justice.detail: About 1,000 animal welfare protesters who tried to gain entry to a beagle breeding and research facility in Wisconsin were turned back by police who fired rubber bullets and pepper spray into the crowd, arresting the group's leader.

Crime & Justice.headline: 56 bodies found dumped at a cemetery in Trinidad and Tobago
Crime & Justice.detail: Police in Trinidad and Tobago discovered 56 bodies, mostly children, that had been dumped at a cemetery.

Crime & Justice.headline: Supreme Court weighs phone searches to find criminals amid complaints of 'digital dragnets'
Crime & Justice.detail: The Supreme Court is considering the extent to which law enforcement can search mobile phones in criminal investigations, amidst concerns about potential 'digital dragnets'.

Crime & Justice.headline: Central African Republic: CPS, the other “headless” court
Crime & Justice.detail: Following the death of its chief prosecutor, the Special Criminal Court (CPS) in Bangui is operating without a chief prosecutor and without an approved budget for 2026, though many cases are ready to trial.

Education.headline: No cap on higher education intake for SPM, STPM holders, says Zambry
Education.detail: The Higher Education Minister of Malaysia stated that there's no fixed limit on student admissions into higher education institutions for Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) and Sijil Tinggi Pelajaran Malaysia (STPM) holders, emphasizing broad access to universities and skills-based fields. Around 100,000 SPM leavers and 75,000 STPM leavers this year have the opportunity to continue their studies.

Education.headline: If education, business and labor come together, Maine can take advantage of AI
Education.detail: An opinion piece discusses how Maine can benefit from AI advancements if education, business, and labor sectors collaborate, focusing on adapting curricula and providing work experiences like internships and apprenticeships. It also highlights the need to address declining reading and math scores to prepare students for an AI-driven world.

Education.headline: CSCU and State Officials Launch Connecticut's Quantum Science Center
Education.detail: Southern Connecticut State University launched the Connecticut State Colleges and Universities Center for Quantum and Nanotechnology (QNT) to drive student opportunity, workforce development, and statewide innovation. Governor Ned Lamont emphasized the importance of making education real and relevant, which will make an enormous difference, from speeding up drug discovery to strengthening industries like insurance and biotech.

Education.headline: IGNOU a pillar of open learning, says V-P at convocation
Education.detail: The Vice-President highlighted IGNOU's role as a key institution for open learning during a convocation ceremony. Details about the specific convocation or initiatives were not provided in the available snippets.

Education.headline: Atlanta private school announces sudden closure
Education.detail: Midtown International School, a private school in Atlanta, has announced a sudden closure, prompting financial questions regarding the circumstances leading to it and why details weren't made public sooner. The closure raises concerns about the school's financial position and communication transparency.

Lifestyle.headline: Skip the strawberries and pair rhubarb with raspberries in this stunning tart
Lifestyle.detail: This raspberry-rhubarb tart is for people who don't like sweet desserts, offering a delightful alternative to traditional strawberry pairings.

Lifestyle.headline: A flight full of strangers celebrated this 2-year-old's cancer recovery
Lifestyle.detail: Cruz Anguiano, who was diagnosed with Stage 4 neuroblastoma in 2025, is now cancer-free and celebrated his recovery with fellow passengers on a flight.

Lifestyle.headline: Standup and author Susan Calman: 'Comedy is an industry full of weirdos – I found my people'
Lifestyle.detail: The comedian and writer Susan Calman discusses overcoming depression, working on death row, and learning to say no in a recent interview.

Lifestyle.headline: Taufik Batisah's wife reveals she underwent six IVF cycles, hopes to help other couples feel less alone
Lifestyle.detail: Sheena Akbal, wife of Taufik Batisah, shares her infertility journey, including six IVF cycles and a miscarriage, on a new podcast, aiming to raise awareness and support for couples facing similar challenges.

Lifestyle.headline: Las Vegas foodie earns Guinness World Record after eating at 28 top restaurants in 24-hour sprint
Lifestyle.detail: A Las Vegas foodie has achieved a Guinness World Record by dining at 28 top restaurants within a 24-hour period.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The GCC has unity, it now needs joint defence and development
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Beyond supporting de-escalation and peace, GCC states need to work towards joint defence, energy security and economic development, as the Gulf states cannot and will not be held hostage under any circumstances, and imposing restrictions on freedom of navigation through the Strait of Hormuz is a flagrant violation of international law.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion: The Corporate Transparency Act sounds harmless. It's not.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Corporate Transparency Act is unconstitutional and confusing, requiring small businesses to report beneficial owners to the Treasury Department, creating compliance costs with little public benefit. The Treasury Department will not enforce it against Americans, but the federal government will amass a database of millions of small businesses, as well as their owners and workers.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Littwin: When Lauren Boebert publicly breaks with Trump, can she leave her MAGA hat on?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Lauren Boebert, a MAGA believer, opposes Trump's Iran war and his support for warrantless spying on Americans, but Republicans are concerned about Trump's poll numbers and the chance they might get wiped out in the midterms. Some suggest this is part of a MAGA breakup, as Republicans are quite concerned about Trump's disappearing poll numbers and the real chance they might get wiped out in the midterms.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Tim Wesley: Why my daughter's family won't come back to America
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A white Pittsburgher discusses how racial issues influence his African American son-in-law's decision to delay moving back to the U.S. due to safety and security concerns for their biracial sons, who will be viewed as Black males and treated differently. He encourages open dialogue about race, emphasizing that racial differences can mean life or death for some citizens.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Not hesitancy but affordability
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Filipinos are becoming more open to vaccination, which is a significant turnaround from a decline in public trust, but prohibitive costs prevent many from getting even a single life-saving shot. Health experts recently observed this encouraging development following a controversy years ago, but barriers remain due to costs.

World.headline: Trump says US negotiators will be in Pakistan on Monday for talks with Iran
World.detail: President Trump announced that U.S. negotiators are scheduled to meet with Iranian officials in Pakistan on Monday in an effort to de-escalate tensions and extend the current ceasefire.

World.headline: Iran closes strait of Hormuz again 'until US lifts blockade'
World.detail: Amid rising tensions, Iran has reimposed strict control over the Strait of Hormuz, stating it will remain closed until the U.S. lifts its blockade on Iranian ports, a move that has been criticized by the U.S. as blackmail.

World.headline: 6 killed after gunman opens fire in Kyiv, Ukrainian officials say
World.detail: In Kyiv, Ukraine, a gunman opened fire, resulting in six fatalities; the head of Ukraine's Interior Ministry reported that the gunman took hostages and engaged in negotiations before being killed.

World.headline: Humanoid robots race past humans in Beijing half-marathon, showing rapid advances
World.detail: In Beijing, humanoid robots outperformed human runners in a half-marathon, demonstrating the rapid advancements in robotics technology.

World.headline: Two Indian men shot dead in Italy while leaving gurdwara
World.detail: Two Indian men were fatally shot in Italy as they were leaving a gurdwara, prompting concern and investigation.

Zimbabwe.headline: Forex inflows surge to US$4,97bn
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe recorded a sharp rise in foreign currency inflows in the first quarter of 2026, as strong performance in key sectors boosted the economy.

Zimbabwe.headline: Good news for consumers as Government slashes fuel prices
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwe Energy Regulatory Authority (Zera) has reduced fuel prices, lowering the cost of both petrol and diesel.

Zimbabwe.headline: Independence weekend delivers busy PSL schedule
Zimbabwe.detail: The Premier Soccer League has a packed schedule this Independence weekend with several exciting matches lined up across the country.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mutsvangwa warns Zanu PF factions to 'wait for 2030'
Zimbabwe.detail: Zanu-PF Spokesperson Christopher Mutsvangwa has cautioned party members against early campaigning for the 2030 elections, advising them to focus on current development initiatives.

Zimbabwe.headline: Nurses call strike for April 20-22 over 'wholly inadequate' salaries
Zimbabwe.detail: Nurses in Zimbabwe have announced a strike from April 20-22 due to dissatisfaction with their current salaries, which they deem insufficient.
